message:
feat(fp-io): SqlConnection::max_identifier_length capability probe (br-cs81 / fd90.26)

Add max_identifier_length() -> Option<usize> capability probe
complementing the existing capability surface (max_param_count,
supports_returning, supports_schemas). Default returns None
(unknown / unlimited). SQLite inherits the default — the engine has
no documented limit. Known caps for backends to override:

  - PostgreSQL: 63
  - MySQL: 64
  - MSSQL: 128
  - Oracle: 30 (pre-12.2) or 128 (12.2+)

Useful for to_sql validation when emitting auto-generated index,
constraint, and column names that could otherwise be silently
truncated or rejected by backend DDL.

Free-fn sql_max_identifier_length(conn) wraps the trait call;
re-exported via the frankenpandas umbrella.

5 new tests cover: SQLite-None default, generic-no-introspection
returns None, PG=63 stub, MySQL=64 stub, MSSQL=128 stub.

323 fp-io tests pass; clippy -D warnings clean across fp-io +
umbrella; ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -71,6 +71,7 @@ pub use fp_io::{
     SqlWriteOptions,
     list_sql_schemas,
     list_sql_tables,
+    sql_max_identifier_length,
     sql_primary_key_columns,
     sql_server_version,
     sql_table_schema,
